name: Ginegar Plastic Products Ltd
excerpt: Ginegar Plastic Products is a world leading producer of advanced films and nets for a wide variety of agricultural applications,...
full_description: Ginegar Plastic Products is a world leading producer of advanced films and nets for a wide variety of agricultural applications, with special emphasis on greenhouse covers, mulches, VIF films and also a range of non-agricultural applications. Since 1969, Ginegar has been combining its expertise in plastics technology, with its agronomic know-how and understanding of the needs of growers worldwide. A long time pioneer in its field, Ginegar was the first to introduce large size multi – layer extruders capable of producing 16-meter wide, 3-layer films for use as greenhouse covers and mulches in the 1980s . Leading the way once again in 2001, Ginegar was the first producer of agricultural polyethylene films to install 5-layer extruders that produced films up to 16 meters wide. These offer significantly enhanced optical, physical and surface functional (anti-drip) performance. Still leading the field today, Ginegar remains an innovator, introducing new solutions and products to meet current and changing needs. It offers both technological expertise and agronomic know-how The Ginegar Product Development Team includes seasoned agronomists with hands-on experience, as well as plastics engineers and technicians. Not only do we know exactly what the grower’s problems are and what he needs to solve them, we also know how to engineer and manufacture it in the best way. Our plastic films, based on polyethylene and its derivatives, including LDPE, HDPE, EVA, LLDPE and PA are manufactured to serve a variety of applications.

name: GrowDirector
excerpt: GrowDirector has engineered horticulture automation solutions
full_description: For over a decade, GrowDirector has engineered horticulture automation solutions to simplify the core jobs of modern cultivation, freeing growers to focus on strategic decisions and crop quality. Developed in close collaboration with growers, our platform is a practical tool built to solve real-world challenges. It delivers plug-and-grow simplicity for any setup, scales on demand as your operation grows, and offers an unmatched ROI with a one-time investment and no subscription fees. Beyond simple automation, we empower you to make smarter crop decisions with AI. Gain data-driven insights, predictive crop disease alerts, and on-demand agronomic recommendations to improve consistency and yield. With proven results across all farm types, GrowDirector is the dedicated partner you need to boost performance, reduce costs, and grow with confidence. Products: GrowDirector 4 PRO Horticulture Automation Platform GrowDirector modules are housed in durable, watertight industrial-grade enclosures engineered to withstand extreme environmental conditions — ensuring high-performance, reliable operation in the most demanding grow environments. The platform is built on a wireless system architecture, allowing maximum installation flexibility and support for distributed setups across any facility. Enclosures are available in multiple sizes and form factors to match system complexity, functionalities, and scale. DryContactDirector Flexible Equipment Control at Scale Control pumps, fans, alarms, and more using dry contact relays — all from a single, streamlined system. Each module supports multiple outputs and can be expanded effortlessly to manage an unlimited number of devices. Setup is fast, integration is seamless, and remote monitoring with customizable alerts ensures full control at any scale. DimmerDirector Scalable Smart Lighting Control Provides precise control over grow lighting systems — including scheduling and brightness adjustments — to match plant growth stages and natural daylight cycles. With remote monitoring, customizable alerts, and an intuitive interface, it offers seamless integration into any size grow operation. Supports both individual and daisy-chained LED lights. Each module can manage up to 200 lights, and the system scales effortlessly to control any number of lights by adding additional modules. SocketDirector Universal electrical device automation Automates fans, heaters, chillers, irrigation pumps, and most electrical devices for efficient control and energy savings. This wireless module can be installed anywhere, offering maximum placement flexibility across your grow environment. Each module supports 2 sockets and scales effortlessly to an unlimited number of devices. Compatible with all major international socket types, it features fast setup, remote monitoring, customizable alerts, and data logging for flexible, distributed power management. HydroDirector Precision Dosing & Water Quality Control Optimizes nutrient delivery and regulates key water parameters — including pH, EC, temperature — to ensure ideal conditions for plant health in hydroponic and fertigation systems. Each module supports 3 pumps and 3 sensors, and the system scales effortlessly with additional modules. Includes customizable alerts, remote monitoring, and intuitive plug-and-play setup. SensorDirector Comprehensive Sensor Support Collects and processes real-time data from a wide range of climate, soil, and water sensors — including temperature, humidity, CO2, EC, pH, water tank level, weather stations, water capacity, and much more. It enables precise environmental control and zone-based automation. Each module supports 4 sensors, with unlimited effortless scalability by adding more units. With fast setup, customizable alerts, remote monitoring and data logging.

name: Hishtil
excerpt: The Israel-based international nurseries organization is a market leader in vegetable pro
full_description: The Israel-based international nurseries organization is a market leader in vegetable propagation. In recent years, besides further expansion of its nursery network, Hishtil is active in sales of nursery’s production and administration technologies and procedures. Hishtil is a world leader in plant propagation and grafting of vegetable plants. Its main product lines are seedlings and young plants of vegetables, ornamental plants and herbs. The company is renowned for being at the forefront of knowledge-based plant propagation. Hishtil is dedicated to providing economic and environmentally friendly horticultural solutions that improve the quality of food. A natural landscape rehabilitation project the company is deeply involved with is an exciting demonstration of this commitment. Hishtil has over 25 hectares of production nursery area across four sites in Israel, plus 25 hectares in 7 international joint ventures in Italy (2), Bosnia, Spain, Turkey and South Africa (2). Yearly production currently totals well over one billion young plants of vegetables, herbs and ornamental plants, of which some 80 million are grafted vegetables. All of Hishtil’s activities comply with the strictest international standards. Global Strategy Hishtil aims to increase global activity to establish a leading international presence for its cutting-edge operations. This includes implementing more facilities that capitalise on its inclination for hot climates. Cooperation with a number of advanced argotech and agrobiz firms is also integral to Hishtil’s global strategy. Technology To maintain its leading position in the field of plant propagation technology, Hishtil’s own Research and Development department (R&D) has close relationships with academic research institutions that assist in developing advanced production methods and technical aids. Sales and markets Hishtil exports propagation materials of seedlings, grafted young plants and rooted/un rooted cuttings from Israel. Its primary export market is Europe, followed by North America. Hishtil is constantly seeking out new export markets. The company is known for its expertise with herbs and all export activity is focused on sales to the hobby market (young potted plants for home gardeners). Food Safety As a responsible propagator of plants for human consumption, Hishtil is dedicated to ensuring suitable hygiene is maintained throughout the production process. • Hazard Analysis: Guarding and monitoring biological, chemical, and physical hazards • Water (bio safety): Microbiology sampling, UV disinfection, CIP procedures, technologies evaluation • Sanitation: Uniquely developed approach, independently approved. • Certified by leading standards: ISO 9001:2008, GLOBALG.A.P, Organic (USDA and IFOAM approved) Hishtil’s vision To become a leading and innovative nursery firm, globally Hishtil’s mission To provide horticultural solutions, utilizing innovative and economic technologies based on cutting edge knowledge. Our aim is to improve the quality of food and the environment.  The company offers its customers around the world, three know-how tracks to choose from. Hishtil’s knowledge division includes two departments: Knowledge Ventures Department – Specializes in establishing nursery projects abroad R&D Department – Focuses on expanding Hishtil’s knowledge

name: Metzer – Drip irrigation system
excerpt: Metzer was established in 1970, by Kibbutz farmers who sought solutions to everyday challenges in its agricultural fields
full_description: Metzer was established in 1970, by Kibbutz farmers who sought solutions to everyday challenges in its agricultural fields. Since then, we have blossomed into a global leader in the field of advanced agricultural technologies. We focus on designing, upgrading and producing a wide range of irrigation driplines and high-speed dripline production lines for our global customers. We also produce multi-layered insulated super pipes for residential, public and industrial use. As an environmentally-conscious company, we work with nature to produce more food, through the use of wastewater for irrigation, and to develop biodegradable products. Our aim is to discover challenges not yet solved and to find solutions through creativity, partnerships and teamwork. The strength of our company lies in its experience, timely response, and adaptation of our products to our client’s needs and challenges. As a company rooted in agriculture, we understand these challenges and make it our mission to offer smart, cost-efficient solutions We want to provide easy incorporation and quick profitability for our customers. Advanced SDI products and technologies, enabling the use of reclaimed and other poor quality water sources as well as mechanized ways of cultivation, are nowadays at the center of our efforts, including affordable sensors to monitor crop development in real time. Among other related fields of research, are bio-degradable irrigation driplines, passive anti-bacterial coatings, nonpoisonous protection against insect and rodent damage, anti-clogging agents, and more.
